description: The Bowery Boys were a nativist, anti-Catholic, and anti-Irish criminal gang based in the Bowery neighborhood of Manhattan, New York City, active from the 1830s to the 1860s. Founded by Mike Walsh and William Poole, the gang consisted of volunteer firemen and engaged in street fighting, knife fighting, assault, murder, robbery, arson, and rioting. They feuded with rival gangs such as the Dead Rabbits and were known for their distinctive uniform of a stovepipe hat, red shirt, and dark trousers tucked into boots.
